title: Scaling Ratios
learningGoal: Learn the core ideas and safe practices for Scaling Ratios.

intro.headline: Scaling Ratios Basics
intro.body: This lesson introduces Scaling Ratios. Read each prompt carefully, then practice with guided questions and feedback.

questions.question: A soup recipe serves 4 people and uses 2 cups of broth. How much broth is needed to serve 8 people?
questions.correct_answer: 4 cups
questions.safety_tip: When doubling a recipe, multiply each ingredient amount by 2.
questions.explanation: Serving 8 people instead of 4 doubles the recipe, so 2 cups of broth becomes 4 cups.

questions.question: A cookie recipe makes 24 cookies and requires 3 cups of flour. How much flour is needed to make 12 cookies?
questions.correct_answer: 1 1/2 cups
questions.safety_tip: Use level measuring cups when measuring flour, and avoid packing it down.
questions.explanation: Making 12 cookies is half of the original batch, so 3 cups of flour must be divided by 2.

questions.question: A recipe serves 6 and uses 1 1/2 teaspoons of salt. How much salt is needed to serve 12?
questions.correct_answer: 3 teaspoons
questions.safety_tip: Measure salt carefully because small scaling errors can noticeably change the flavor.
questions.explanation: Serving 12 instead of 6 doubles the recipe, so 1 1/2 teaspoons becomes 3 teaspoons.

questions.question: A pasta recipe serves 8 and uses 2 pounds of pasta. How much pasta is needed to serve 2 people?
questions.correct_answer: 1/2 pound
questions.safety_tip: Keep the same ingredient-to-serving ratio when reducing a recipe.
questions.explanation: Two servings are one-fourth of eight servings, so 2 pounds divided by 4 equals 1/2 pound.

questions.question: A pancake recipe makes 10 pancakes using 2 eggs. How many eggs are needed to make 20 pancakes?
questions.correct_answer: 4 eggs
questions.safety_tip: Wash your hands and any surfaces that come into contact with raw eggs.
questions.explanation: Making 20 pancakes doubles the original recipe, so 2 eggs becomes 4 eggs.

questions.question: A sauce recipe makes 3 cups and uses 3/4 cup of tomato paste. How much tomato paste is needed to make 1 cup of sauce?
questions.correct_answer: 1/4 cup
questions.safety_tip: Divide every ingredient by the same number when reducing the total yield.
questions.explanation: One cup is one-third of the original 3-cup yield, so 3/4 cup divided by 3 equals 1/4 cup.

questions.question: A muffin recipe makes 12 muffins and uses 1/2 cup of sugar. How much sugar is needed to make 18 muffins?
questions.correct_answer: 3/4 cup
questions.safety_tip: Find the scaling factor before changing any ingredient amounts.
questions.explanation: Eighteen muffins are 1.5 times the original batch, so 1/2 cup multiplied by 1.5 equals 3/4 cup.

questions.question: A bread recipe uses 4 cups of flour and 2 cups of water. If you use 6 cups of flour, how much water keeps the same ratio?
questions.correct_answer: 3 cups
questions.safety_tip: Maintain the original flour-to-water ratio to avoid dough that is too dry or too wet.
questions.explanation: The recipe uses 1 cup of water for every 2 cups of flour, so 6 cups of flour requires 3 cups of water.

questions.question: A casserole recipe serves 5 and uses 10 ounces of cheese. How much cheese is needed to serve 15?
questions.correct_answer: 30 ounces
questions.safety_tip: Keep dairy refrigerated until you are ready to prepare the dish.
questions.explanation: Serving 15 people is three times the original yield, so 10 ounces multiplied by 3 equals 30 ounces.

questions.question: A salad dressing recipe uses 3 tablespoons of oil for every 1 tablespoon of vinegar. How much oil is needed if you use 4 tablespoons of vinegar?
questions.correct_answer: 12 tablespoons
questions.safety_tip: Measure both ingredients with the same type of measuring spoon to keep the ratio consistent.
questions.explanation: The ratio is 3 tablespoons of oil for each tablespoon of vinegar, so 4 tablespoons of vinegar requires 12 tablespoons of oil.

recap: Review the main concepts from Scaling Ratios.
Focus on accuracy first, then speed.
Use the safety tips during real kitchen practice.

title: Emergency Flavor Tuning
learningGoal: Learn the core ideas and safe practices for Emergency Flavor Tuning.

intro.headline: Emergency Flavor Tuning Basics
intro.body: This lesson introduces Emergency Flavor Tuning. Read each prompt carefully, then practice with guided questions and feedback.

questions.question: A pot of vegetable soup tastes bland even though the ingredients are fully cooked. What should you try first?
options: Add a small amount of salt, stir, and taste again
Add an entire cup of sugar
Boil the soup until half of it evaporates
Add several tablespoons of baking soda
questions.correct_answer: Add a small amount of salt, stir, and taste again
questions.safety_tip: Add seasoning gradually because you can add more salt, but removing excess salt is difficult.
questions.explanation: A small amount of salt can bring out flavors that are already present. Stirring and tasting after each addition prevents over-seasoning.

questions.question: A tomato sauce tastes too acidic. Which adjustment is most appropriate?
options: Add a small pinch of sugar and taste again
Add a large amount of vinegar
Add several spoonfuls of raw flour
questions.correct_answer: Add a small pinch of sugar and taste again
questions.safety_tip: Use only a small amount of sugar at a time so the sauce does not become noticeably sweet.
questions.explanation: A small amount of sugar can balance excessive acidity without completely changing the flavor of the tomato sauce.

questions.question: A curry tastes too spicy for your guests. What is the best way to reduce the perceived heat?
options: Stir in coconut milk or plain yogurt
Boil the curry without a lid for 20 minutes
questions.correct_answer: Stir in coconut milk or plain yogurt
questions.safety_tip: Lower the heat before adding yogurt so it is less likely to curdle.
questions.explanation: Fat and dairy proteins can soften the sensation of chili heat, while adding more liquid also spreads the spicy compounds through a larger volume.

questions.question: A stew tastes too salty. Which emergency adjustment is most effective?
options: Add more unsalted broth and other unseasoned ingredients
Cook the stew until most of the liquid evaporates
questions.correct_answer: Add more unsalted broth and other unseasoned ingredients
questions.safety_tip: Taste the broth before adding it to confirm that it is unsalted or low in sodium.
questions.explanation: Adding unseasoned ingredients increases the total volume and dilutes the excess salt. Evaporation would concentrate the salt and make the problem worse.

questions.question: A cream sauce tastes overly rich and heavy. What can you add to brighten and balance it?
options: A small squeeze of lemon juice
questions.correct_answer: A small squeeze of lemon juice
questions.safety_tip: Remove the sauce from high heat and add acid gradually to reduce the risk of curdling.
questions.explanation: A small amount of acid can cut through richness and make a heavy sauce taste more balanced.

questions.question: A homemade salad dressing tastes too sour. Which adjustment is most appropriate?
options: Whisk in a little more oil and a small amount of sweetener
questions.correct_answer: Whisk in a little more oil and a small amount of sweetener
questions.safety_tip: Make small adjustments and taste between additions so the dressing remains balanced.
questions.explanation: Additional oil dilutes the acid, while a small amount of sweetener can soften the perception of sourness.

questions.question: A pan sauce tastes bitter because the garlic was slightly overcooked. What is the best immediate response?
options: Transfer the sauce to a clean pan and balance it with a little fat or sweetness
Scrape the darkest burned pieces into the sauce
Reduce the sauce until it becomes thicker
questions.correct_answer: Transfer the sauce to a clean pan and balance it with a little fat or sweetness
questions.safety_tip: Do not scrape burned residue from the bottom of the pan because it can intensify bitterness.
questions.explanation: Moving the sauce prevents further contact with burned residue, and a small amount of fat or sweetness may soften mild bitterness.

questions.question: A bean chili tastes flat even after enough salt has been added. Which ingredient could provide needed brightness?
options: A small splash of lime juice
questions.correct_answer: A small splash of lime juice
questions.safety_tip: Add acidic ingredients near the end and taste after each small addition.
questions.explanation: Acid can brighten flavors and add contrast when a dish tastes flat despite having enough salt.

questions.question: A sauce has become too sweet. What is the best way to rebalance it?
options: Add a small amount of acid and a pinch of salt
Reduce it until the sugar becomes more concentrated
questions.correct_answer: Add a small amount of acid and a pinch of salt
questions.safety_tip: Add vinegar or citrus juice a few drops at a time to avoid making the sauce too sour.
questions.explanation: Acid and salt can reduce the perception of excessive sweetness and restore balance without adding more sugar.

questions.question: A finished rice dish tastes one-dimensional but is already salty enough. Which addition is most likely to improve its flavor?
options: Fresh herbs and a little citrus zest
questions.correct_answer: Fresh herbs and a little citrus zest
questions.safety_tip: Wash fresh herbs and citrus thoroughly before chopping or zesting them.
questions.explanation: Fresh herbs add aroma, while citrus zest adds brightness without significantly increasing saltiness or changing the dish's texture.

recap: Review the main concepts from Emergency Flavor Tuning.
Focus on accuracy first, then speed.
Use the safety tips during real kitchen practice.

title: Texture Fixes
learningGoal: Learn the core ideas and safe practices for Texture Fixes.

intro.headline: Texture Fixes Basics
intro.body: This lesson introduces Texture Fixes. Read each prompt carefully, then practice with guided questions and feedback.

questions.question: A soup is much thinner than intended. What is the best way to thicken it quickly without creating lumps?
options: Stir in a slurry made from cornstarch and cold water
Sprinkle dry flour directly onto the soup
Add several tablespoons of cooking oil
questions.correct_answer: Stir in a slurry made from cornstarch and cold water
questions.safety_tip: Mix the cornstarch with cold water before adding it, then stir the hot soup continuously.
questions.explanation: A cornstarch slurry disperses smoothly and thickens the soup as it heats. Adding dry starch directly can create lumps.

questions.question: Mashed potatoes have become too watery. Which adjustment is most likely to improve their texture?
options: Stir in instant potato flakes a little at a time
Mix in raw cornstarch without heating
questions.correct_answer: Stir in instant potato flakes a little at a time
questions.safety_tip: Add the flakes gradually because too much can make the potatoes dry or pasty.
questions.explanation: Instant potato flakes absorb excess moisture and thicken mashed potatoes without adding a strong new flavor.

questions.question: A cooked sauce is too thick. What is the best way to thin it while maintaining control of the texture?
options: Whisk in a small amount of warm liquid at a time
Add a large amount of cold water all at once
Cook the sauce longer without a lid
questions.correct_answer: Whisk in a small amount of warm liquid at a time
questions.safety_tip: Add liquid gradually and keep the heat moderate to prevent splashing or sudden separation.
questions.explanation: Adding warm liquid slowly allows you to loosen the sauce evenly without making it unexpectedly watery.

questions.question: A cream sauce looks grainy and has started to separate. What should you try first?
options: Remove it from the heat and whisk in a small amount of warm cream
Boil it rapidly for several minutes
questions.correct_answer: Remove it from the heat and whisk in a small amount of warm cream
questions.safety_tip: High heat can cause dairy sauces to separate, so lower or remove the heat before attempting a repair.
questions.explanation: Reducing the heat stops further separation, and gently whisking in warm cream may help restore a smoother emulsion.

questions.question: A batch of cooked rice is slightly too wet. What is the best immediate fix?
options: Spread it on a tray or cook it briefly uncovered over low heat
Add more water and cover it tightly
Stir it constantly over high heat
Mix in uncooked rice and serve immediately
questions.correct_answer: Spread it on a tray or cook it briefly uncovered over low heat
questions.safety_tip: Keep the heat low and watch the rice closely so the bottom does not burn.
questions.explanation: Exposing the rice to air or gentle heat allows excess moisture to evaporate without adding more liquid.

questions.question: A gravy contains small lumps after thickening. What is the best way to improve its texture?
options: Whisk thoroughly or strain it through a fine-mesh strainer
Let the gravy cool without stirring
questions.correct_answer: Whisk thoroughly or strain it through a fine-mesh strainer
questions.safety_tip: Hold the strainer securely and pour hot gravy carefully to prevent burns.
questions.explanation: Whisking can break up small lumps, while straining physically removes lumps and produces a smoother gravy.

questions.question: Cookie dough is dry and crumbly and will not hold together. What is the best adjustment?
options: Add a small amount of milk or water, one teaspoon at a time
Add another full cup of flour
Bake the dough for twice as long
Add several tablespoons of baking powder
questions.correct_answer: Add a small amount of milk or water, one teaspoon at a time
questions.safety_tip: Add liquid slowly because too much can make the dough sticky and cause the cookies to spread.
questions.explanation: A small amount of liquid can restore enough moisture for the dough to hold together without changing it dramatically.

questions.question: A homemade salad dressing separates immediately after mixing. What should you do?
options: Whisk vigorously while slowly adding the oil
Add all the oil at once without stirring
Heat the dressing until it boils
Add dry flour directly to the dressing
questions.correct_answer: Whisk vigorously while slowly adding the oil
questions.safety_tip: Use a stable bowl and pour the oil in a thin stream to maintain control while whisking.
questions.explanation: Slowly adding oil while whisking helps break the oil into tiny droplets and creates a more stable emulsion.

questions.question: A custard is slightly lumpy after cooking. Which method is most likely to make it smoother?
options: Press it through a fine-mesh strainer while it is still warm
Boil it again over high heat
questions.correct_answer: Press it through a fine-mesh strainer while it is still warm
questions.safety_tip: Handle the warm custard carefully and use a heat-resistant utensil when pressing it through the strainer.
questions.explanation: Straining removes small pieces of overcooked egg and produces a smoother custard texture.

questions.question: A casserole topping is soft instead of crisp. What is the best way to improve it near the end of cooking?
options: Broil it briefly while watching it closely
Lower the oven temperature and add more sauce
questions.correct_answer: Broil it briefly while watching it closely
questions.safety_tip: Broilers can burn food quickly, so remain nearby and check the topping frequently.
questions.explanation: Brief exposure to intense top heat can brown and crisp the surface without significantly overcooking the entire casserole.

recap: Review the main concepts from Texture Fixes.
Focus on accuracy first, then speed.
Use the safety tips during real kitchen practice.
